_meta.title: Pulse Of Raga
_meta.subtitle: A Carnatic raga explorer · 8 hero ragas, scales, master recordings, and ready-made Suno fusion seeds

ragas.name: Mohanam
ragas.tagline: The major pentatonic — sunlit, open, instantly singable.
ragas.mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (often morning per modern convention)
ragas.rasa_note: Pentatonic janya of Harikambhoji; equivalent to Hindustani Bhoop / Western major pentatonic; Tyagaraja's 'Nannu Palimpa', 'Mohana Rama'.

fusion.prompt: lo-fi hip-hop fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Mohanam. The lead melody strictly follows raga Mohanam: ascending S R2 G3 P D2 S, descending S D2 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full lo-fi hip-hop production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of lo-fi hip-hop. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming. Around 82 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Mohanam is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

festival / big-room fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Mohanam. The lead melody strictly follows raga Mohanam: ascending S R2 G3 P D2 S, descending S D2 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Underneath, full festival / big-room production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of festival / big-room. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming. Around 128 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Mohanam is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

ragas.name: Hamsadhwani
ragas.tagline: The swan-song raga — every Carnatic concert opens here.
ragas.mood: joyous, auspicious, energetic — 'the sound of swans'
ragas.rasa_note: Created by the Carnatic composer Ramaswami Dikshitar (1735–1817), father of Muthuswami Dikshitar, per Wikipedia on Hamsadhvani; brought into Hindustani music by Aman Ali Khan of the Bhendibazaar gharana; the quintessential concert opener; Dikshitar's 'Vatapi Ganapatim', Tyagaraja's 'Raghu Nayaka'.

fusion.prompt: drum and bass fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Hamsadhwani. The lead melody strictly follows raga Hamsadhwani: ascending S R2 G3 P N3 S, descending S N3 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full drum and bass production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of drum and bass. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: joyous, auspicious, energetic — 'the sound of swans'. Around 174 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Hamsadhwani is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

nu-disco fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Hamsadhwani. The lead melody strictly follows raga Hamsadhwani: ascending S R2 G3 P N3 S, descending S N3 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full nu-disco production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of nu-disco. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: joyous, auspicious, energetic — 'the sound of swans'. Around 112 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Hamsadhwani is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

ragas.name: Bhairavi
ragas.tagline: Boundless devotion and pathos — the raga of M.S. Subbulakshmi.
ragas.mood: deep devotion and tranquility with cosmic earthiness
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (different from Hindustani Bhairavi)
ragas.rasa_note: Bhashanga janya of Natabhairavi; uses both dhaivatas; Pacchimiriyam Adiappayya's 'Viriboni' ata-tala varnam; Dikshitar's 'Balagopala'; Syama Sastri's swarajatis.

fusion.prompt: sufi electronic fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Bhairavi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Bhairavi: ascending S R2 G2 M1 P D2 N2 S, descending S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full sufi electronic production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of sufi electronic. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: deep devotion and tranquility with cosmic earthiness. Around 100 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

cinematic ambient fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Bhairavi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Bhairavi: ascending S R2 G2 M1 P D2 N2 S, descending S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Underneath, full cinematic ambient production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of cinematic ambient. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

ragas.name: Mechakalyani
ragas.tagline: The queen of ragas — radiant, majestic, evening-bright.
ragas.mood: majestic, auspicious, graceful; 'mother who dispels fear'
ragas.rasa_note: 65th mela (Kalyani / Yaman); the 'queen of ragas'; encapsulates bhakti, shringara, vatsalya; 'Nidhi Chala Sukhama' (Tyagaraja), 'Kamalambam Bhajare' (Dikshitar).

ragas.name: Dheerasankarabharanam
ragas.tagline: The king of ragas — the Carnatic major scale itself.
ragas.mood: majestic, sublime, uplifting; conveys all nine rasas in skilled hands
ragas.rasa_note: 29th mela; the 'king of ragas'; equivalent to Western major / Bilaval thaat; 'Akshayalinga Vibho' (Dikshitar), 'Swara Raga Sudha Rasa' (Tyagaraja).

The lead melody strictly follows raga Dheerasankarabharanam: ascending S R2 G3 M1 P D2 N3 S, descending S N3 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full epic cinematic production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of epic cinematic. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: majestic, sublime, uplifting; conveys all nine rasas in skilled hands. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it.

ragas.name: Hanumatodi
ragas.tagline: Deep, complex pathos — the connoisseur's raga.
ragas.mood: deep pathos with profound devotion; heavy and emotionally complex
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (often rendered as a main raga in concerts)
ragas.rasa_note: 8th mela (Thodi); rakti, sarva-svara-gamaka raga; per Wikipedia, 'Thyagaraja alone has composed about 32 compositions in this raga with each composition starting at every single note of the three octaves'; 'Thaye Yashoda' (Oothukkadu) is iconic.

fusion.prompt: downtempo trip-hop fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Hanumatodi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Hanumatodi: ascending S R1 G2 M1 P D1 N2 S, descending S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R1 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full downtempo trip-hop production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of downtempo trip-hop. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: deep pathos with profound devotion; heavy and emotionally complex. Around 90 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it.

cinematic ambient fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Hanumatodi. ragas.name: Kambodhi
ragas.tagline: Regal and oceanic — grandeur with feminine grace.
ragas.mood: grand, majestic, regal devotion with feminine grace
ragas.time_of_day: evening / first quarter of night (older treatise per Sivatattvaratnakara)
ragas.rasa_note: Janya of Harikambhoji; one of the 'Big Five' major ragas; Tyagaraja's 'Sri Subrahmanyaya Namaste' (Pancharatna), 'Evari Mata', Dikshitar's 'Sri Subrahmanyaya'.

fusion.prompt: synthwave fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Kambodhi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Kambodhi: ascending S R2 G3 M1 P D2 S, descending S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full synthwave production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of synthwave. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: grand, majestic, regal devotion with feminine grace. Around 104 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it.

organic house fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Kambodhi. Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. ragas.name: Kanada
ragas.tagline: Grave and tender — compassion welling up from love.
ragas.mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love
ragas.rasa_note: Janya of Kharaharapriya; tri-sthayi raga with gravitas; Tyagaraja's 'Sukhi Yevvaro', 'Sri Narada'; Oothukkadu's 'Alaipayudhe Kanna'.

fusion.prompt: melodic dubstep fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Kanada. The lead melody strictly follows raga Kanada: ascending S R2 G2 M1 D2 N2 S, descending S N2 P M1 G2 M1 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full melodic dubstep production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of melodic dubstep. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love. Around 140 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Honour the raga's vakra (zig-zag) phrasing — the note order is deliberately crooked, not a straight scale. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

downtempo fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Kanada. The lead melody strictly follows raga Kanada: ascending S R2 G2 M1 D2 N2 S, descending S N2 P M1 G2 M1 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full downtempo production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of downtempo. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love. Around 92 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Honour the raga's vakra (zig-zag) phrasing — the note order is deliberately crooked, not a straight scale. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.
